items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Drone attack on Oman raises the stakes
items.summary: Explosion halts crude loading at Oman's Mina al Fahal in conflict escalation PBOC resumes injections after two-day pause that forced banks to deploy idle cash Nasdaq analysis today shows market started selling quite hard for this time of day RBA's Hauser due to speak, hawkish tilt expected after Harper's inflation warning PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8157 (vs. estimate at 6.7735) South Korean markets hammered. KOSPI off 6%, won at a 17 year low vs. USD Japan renews yen intervention threat as reserves fall by record amount in May Fake news? : Trump officials in talks to take government equity stakes in US AI giants Japan fin min Katayama said ready to respond to FX. Yen shrugs it off. Iran threatens US bases and Hormuz as war talks drag with no deal in sight Japan's real wages rise for fourth month, strengthening the BOJ rate-hike case SpaceX faces full S&P 500 wait as index giant rejects fast-track entry rules Goldman tells clients to buy US equity dips even as risk appetite hits highest since 2021 ICYMI: OPEC says oil demand remains strong despite Hormuz, Mid East conflict surging price All crypto eyes on Strategy's Monday disclosure as bitcoin seeks a floor after brutal week investingLive Americas market news wrap: Another comeback for stocks as Trump talks peace Dow closes at a record high. S&P is higher, and the NASDAQ index is down on the day Summary: Explosion reported near Oman's Mina al Fahal crude terminal on the Gulf of Oman; cause unclear but an alleged drone attack has been cited, extending conflict risk beyond the Strait of Hormuz to open-water export infrastructure, oil tracked broadly sideways Japan April real wages rose for a fourth consecutive month, the longest such streak in five years; wages and household spending both beat forecasts, strengthening the BOJ's case for a rate hike at its June 15-16 meeting Finance Minister Katayama renewed yen intervention warnings with USD/JPY around 160; the pair barely moved as dollar trading was broadly subdued ahead of US payrolls later Friday Japanese equities fell, with souring tech sentiment cited as the primary drag South Korea's KOSPI deepened losses, falling 6+%, as the Korean won hit its weakest level against the dollar since March 2009; a Labour Minister suggestion that Samsung and AI-boom beneficiaries share excess profits with workers echoed the kind of official commentary that triggered a previous KOSPI slide Notus reported that senior Trump administration officials have held discussions with major AI companies including OpenAI about voluntarily ceding equity stakes to the government, with returns potentially distributed to American citizens as an AI dividend Hong Kong equities fell after Beijing moved to tighten cross-border capital outflow controls; mainland Chinese stocks held up better, supported by the PBOC resuming open market operations Asian markets closed out the week on a cautious note, with South Korean equities bearing the sharpest losses as a confluence of domestic political signals and broader risk-off sentiment drove the KOSPI down >6% at one stage. The Korean won deepened its slide against the dollar, hitting its weakest level since March 2009, as selling pressure intensified through the session. The proximate trigger was a suggestion from South Korea's Labour Minister that Samsung and other companies riding the AI boom should consider distributing a share of the excess profits those gains have produced. It was the kind of official commentary that has rattled Korean markets before, and traders did not wait to find out whether it would be followed by policy action. The AI profit-sharing theme found an unlikely parallel in a report from US outlet Notus, which revealed that senior Trump administration officials have held preliminary discussions with major artificial intelligence companies, including OpenAI, about the government acquiring voluntary equity stakes in their firms. Under the concept as described, those shareholdings could eventually generate returns distributed directly to American citizens in the form of an AI dividend. The talks are at an early stage and the legal pathway remains unclear, but the emergence of the idea from both Washington and Seoul in the same session underlines how politically charged the distribution of AI wealth is becoming. Elsewhere in the region, an explosion near Oman's Mina al Fahal crude terminal added a new dimension to the conflict risk picture. The terminal sits on the Gulf of Oman, outside the Persian Gulf, and an alleged drone attack there, if confirmed, would mark a visible extension of infrastructure targeting beyond the Strait of Hormuz into open-water export facilities. Oman's role as a back-channel intermediary in US-Iran negotiations gives the incident additional diplomatic weight, and it drew immediate scrutiny from energy traders, shipping markets, and analysts tracking the conflict's geographic spread. Oil itself tracked broadly sideways, suggesting markets are still waiting for confirmation of cause and impact before repricing. In Japan, the data flow was constructive. Real wages rose for a fourth consecutive month in April, the longest rising streak in five years, with both nominal earnings and household spending beating forecasts by a meaningful margin. The dataset strengthens the Bank of Japan's hand ahead of its June 15-16 policy meeting, where a rate hike signal is increasingly in play. Finance Minister Katayama renewed her warning that Tokyo stands ready to take decisive action on the yen, with USD/JPY hovering around 160, though the pair barely moved as the dollar was broadly subdued in pre-payroll trade. Japanese equities fell, with tech sentiment souring in the wake of the KOSPI selloff and broader caution around the AI trade. Hong Kong stocks declined after Beijing moved to tighten controls on cross-border capital outflows, while mainland Chinese markets held up more steadily, supported by the PBOC's return to open market operations after a two-day pause that had been read as a deliberate push to redirect idle bank reserves toward the real economy. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 阿曼米纳法哈尔港发生爆炸，原油装船作业暂停，冲突持续升级。中国人民银行在暂停两天后恢复注资，此前暂停曾迫使银行动用闲置现金。纳斯达克今日分析显示，市场在这一时段出现较大幅度的抛售。澳大利亚储备银行的豪泽即将发表讲话，在哈珀发出通胀警告后，预计其将采取鹰派立场。中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8157 （市场预期为6.7735）韩国股市重挫。KOSPI指数下跌6%，韩元兑美元汇率跌至17年低点。日本因5月外汇储备创纪录下降，再次威胁干预日元汇率。假新闻？：特朗普政府官员正商讨由政府入股美国人工智能巨头。日本财务大臣片山表示准备应对汇率波动。日元对此反应平淡。 伊朗威胁美国军事基地和霍尔木兹海峡，而战争谈判陷入僵局，尚无达成协议的迹象 日本实际工资连续第四个月上涨，强化了日本央行加息的理由 SpaceX面临全面等待标普500指数的局面，因该指数巨头拒绝了快速入围规则 高盛建议客户逢低买入美股，尽管市场风险偏好已升至2021年以来最高水平 错过了吗： 欧佩克称尽管霍尔木兹海峡及中东冲突推高油价，但石油需求依然强劲 加密货币市场聚焦Strategy周一披露，比特币在惨烈一周后寻求底部支撑 investingLive美洲市场新闻综述：特朗普谈和平，股市再度反弹，道指收于历史新高。 标普指数上涨，纳斯达克指数当日下跌 摘要：据报道，阿曼湾的阿曼米纳·法哈尔原油终端附近发生爆炸； 起因尚不明确，但有消息称系无人机袭击所致，这将冲突风险从霍尔木兹海峡延伸至公海出口基础设施，油价整体呈横盘整理态势 日本4月实际工资连续第四个月上涨，创五年来最长连涨纪录；工资和家庭支出均超出预期，强化了日本央行在6月15日至16日会议上加息的理由 随着美元兑日元汇率徘徊在160附近，财务大臣片山重明再次发出日元干预警告； 受周五晚间美国非农数据公布前美元交易普遍清淡影响，该汇率几乎未见波动；日本股市下跌，科技板块情绪恶化被视为主要拖累因素；韩国KOSPI指数跌幅扩大，下跌逾6%，韩元兑美元汇率跌至2009年3月以来最低水平； 韩国劳工部长建议三星及人工智能热潮的受益企业与员工分享超额利润，此类官方言论曾引发此前KOSPI指数的下跌；Notus报道称，特朗普政府高级官员已与主要人工智能公司进行了磋商 包括OpenAI在内的多家公司正考虑自愿向政府转让股权，所得收益或将作为“人工智能红利”分配给美国公民；在中央政府收紧跨境资本流出管控后，香港股市下跌； 受中国人民银行恢复公开市场操作支撑，中国内地股市表现相对稳健；亚洲股市本周收盘时情绪谨慎，韩国股市跌幅最大，国内政治信号与整体避险情绪叠加，一度导致KOSPI指数下跌逾6%。随着盘中抛售压力加剧，韩元兑美元汇率跌势加剧，触及2009年3月以来的最低水平。直接导火索是韩国劳动部长建议三星及其他借AI热潮获利的企业，应考虑分配部分由此产生的超额利润。此类官方言论此前曾多次引发韩国市场动荡，交易员们并未等待政策是否会随之出台。 关于人工智能利润分享的议题，在美国媒体Notus的一份报道中意外找到了呼应。该报道披露，特朗普政府的高级官员已与包括OpenAI在内的主要人工智能公司进行了初步讨论，商讨政府以自愿方式收购这些公司股权的事宜。根据报道所述的构想，这些股权最终产生的收益将以“人工智能股息”的形式直接分配给美国公民。谈判尚处于初期阶段，法律路径尚不明确，但华盛顿和首尔在同一会议上同时提出这一构想，凸显了人工智能财富分配问题正变得多么具有政治敏感性。在该地区其他地方，阿曼米纳·法哈尔原油终端附近发生的一起爆炸事件，为冲突风险图景增添了新的维度。该终端位于波斯湾外的阿曼湾，如果据称的无人机袭击得到证实，这将标志着针对基础设施的袭击范围已从霍尔木兹海峡延伸至公海出口设施。 阿曼作为美伊谈判中幕后渠道中介所扮演的角色，使此次事件具有更重的外交分量，并立即引起了能源交易商、航运市场以及关注冲突地域扩散情况的分析师的密切关注。油价整体呈横盘整理态势，表明市场仍在等待事件原因和影响的确认，才进行重新定价。在日本，经济数据表现积极。4月实际工资连续第四个月上涨，创下五年来的最长连涨纪录，名义收入和家庭支出均大幅超出预期。这一数据增强了日本央行在6月15日至16日政策会议前的底气，届时市场对加息信号的预期正日益升温。尽管美元在非农数据公布前的交易中整体表现疲软，美元兑日元汇率仍徘徊在160附近，日本财务大臣片山重明再次警告称，日本政府已准备好对日元采取果断行动。受韩国KOSPI指数抛售以及市场对AI板块普遍持谨慎态度影响，科技股情绪转差，日股因此下跌。 在中央政府采取措施收紧跨境资本外流管控后，香港股市出现下跌；而中国内地股市则表现更为稳健，这得益于中国人民银行在暂停两天后重启公开市场操作——此前市场普遍认为，此次暂停是旨在将闲置的银行储备资金引导至实体经济的刻意之举。本文作者为investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹。

KOSPI off 6%, won at a 17 year low vs. USD Japan renews yen intervention threat as reserves fall by record amount in May Fake news? : Trump officials in talks to take government equity stakes in US AI giants Japan fin min Katayama said ready to respond to FX. Yen shrugs it off. S&P is higher, and the NASDAQ index is down on the day Summary: Explosion reported near Oman's Mina al Fahal crude terminal on the Gulf of Oman; cause unclear but an alleged drone attack has been cited, extending conflict risk beyond the Strait of Hormuz to open-water export infrastructure, oil tracked broadly sideways Japan April real wages rose for a fourth consecutive month, the longest such streak in five years; wages and household spending both beat forecasts, strengthening the BOJ's case for a rate hike at its June 15-16 meeting Finance Minister Katayama renewed yen intervention warnings with USD/JPY around 160; the pair barely moved as dollar trading was broadly subdued ahead of US payrolls later Friday Japanese equities fell, with souring tech sentiment cited as the primary drag South Korea's KOSPI deepened losses, falling 6+%, as the Korean won hit its weakest level against the dollar since March 2009; a Labour Minister suggestion that Samsung and AI-boom beneficiaries share excess profits with workers echoed the kind of official commentary that triggered a previous KOSPI slide Notus reported that senior Trump administration officials have held discussions with major AI companies including OpenAI about voluntarily ceding equity stakes to the government, with returns potentially distributed to American citizens as an AI dividend Hong Kong equities fell after Beijing moved to tighten cross-border capital outflow controls; mainland Chinese stocks held up better, supported by the PBOC resuming open market operations Asian markets closed out the week on a cautious note, with South Korean equities bearing the sharpest losses as a confluence of domestic political signals and broader risk-off sentiment drove the KOSPI down >6% at one stage. It was the kind of official commentary that has rattled Korean markets before, and traders did not wait to find out whether it would be followed by policy action. The AI profit-sharing theme found an unlikely parallel in a report from US outlet Notus, which revealed that senior Trump administration officials have held preliminary discussions with major artificial intelligence companies, including OpenAI, about the government acquiring voluntary equity stakes in their firms. Under the concept as described, those shareholdings could eventually generate returns distributed directly to American citizens in the form of an AI dividend. The talks are at an early stage and the legal pathway remains unclear, but the emergence of the idea from both Washington and Seoul in the same session underlines how politically charged the distribution of AI wealth is becoming. Elsewhere in the region, an explosion near Oman's Mina al Fahal crude terminal added a new dimension to the conflict risk picture. The terminal sits on the Gulf of Oman, outside the Persian Gulf, and an alleged drone attack there, if confirmed, would mark a visible extension of infrastructure targeting beyond the Strait of Hormuz into open-water export facilities. Oman's role as a back-channel intermediary in US-Iran negotiations gives the incident additional diplomatic weight, and it drew immediate scrutiny from energy traders, shipping markets, and analysts tracking the conflict's geographic spread. Oil itself tracked broadly sideways, suggesting markets are still waiting for confirmation of cause and impact before repricing. In Japan, the data flow was constructive. Real wages rose for a fourth consecutive month in April, the longest rising streak in five years, with both nominal earnings and household spending beating forecasts by a meaningful margin. The dataset strengthens the Bank of Japan's hand ahead of its June 15-16 policy meeting, where a rate hike signal is increasingly in play. Finance Minister Katayama renewed her warning that Tokyo stands ready to take decisive action on the yen, with USD/JPY hovering around 160, though the pair barely moved as the dollar was broadly subdued in pre-payroll trade. Japanese equities fell, with tech sentiment souring in the wake of the KOSPI selloff and broader caution around the AI trade. Hong Kong stocks declined after Beijing moved to tighten controls on cross-border capital outflows, while mainland Chinese markets held up more steadily, supported by the PBOC's return to open market operations after a two-day pause that had been read as a deliberate push to redirect idle bank reserves toward the real economy. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Israel Lebanon ceasefire, Hezbollah permitting
items.summary: AI infrastructure spend on track to rival world's largest economies, Goldman says ICYMI - Kuwait targets 70% oil output recovery within weeks of Hormuz reopening RBA governor faces Senate grilling as CBA sees rates on hold after three straight hikes Australian April Exports leap higher at +7.2% m/m (vs. -2.7% prior) ANZ commodity index rises on tight supply, but NZD gains clip export returns PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8203 (vs. estimate at 6.7770) The Bitcoin puke continues. Back near $62K Trump told aides he would end Iran ceasefire if Tehran kills US troops, WSJ cite officials Trump official says US food supply, safety, not in danger from 'fully contained' screwworm Oil update, price a little lower. New Israel-Lebanon ceasefire hopes. Here we go again. Israel, Lebanon agree ceasefire framework, but Hezbollah holds the key. IEA stockpile warning and deeper crude draw add fuel to Wednesday's oil rally ICYMI - Fed dissenter Logan raises alarm on inflation ahead of Warsh's first meeting US House of Representatives votes against Trump's war. The vote means nothing. JPMorgan sees Strait reopening in June but leaves the hard question unanswered investingLive Americas market news wrap: Dollar firms as war angst creeps in Trump pumping the market after the close - says Iran pretty close to signing Broadcom shares slide after earnings Summary: The Trump administration announced Israel and Lebanon agreed to implement a ceasefire and establish security zones, contingent on a complete halt to Hezbollah fire and evacuation of its operatives from the South Litani sector; a previous ceasefire agreed last month had failed to hold Trump suggested a deal with Iran could come "over the weekend" while reports indicated he privately told aides he would not resume all-out war unless US troops are killed The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution to block further strikes on Iran, the first such vote of the conflict; analysts described it as largely symbolic Asian stocks fell Thursday morning, with Seoul and Tokyo both sharply lower as renewed Iran tensions drove a risk-off shift; the won fell sharply USD/JPY drifted back below 160; major FX moves were otherwise subdued Chinese analysts said PBoC liquidity would remain stable and ample in June despite the central bank skipping open market operations for a second day The New World screwworm, a parasitic fly lethal to warm-blooded animals, was found in a calf in Texas, raising alarm over the US cattle herd Oil prices edged lower on Wednesday as a flurry of contradictory signals on the US-Iran conflict left markets unable to commit to a direction, with Trump's weekend deal hint doing just enough to take the edge off what had been a firmly risk-off session. The day's dominant thread was a collision of escalation and diplomacy that has become the defining rhythm of this conflict. Iran struck Kuwait's international airport the previous day, injuring dozens and prompting US military strikes near the Strait of Hormuz. Against that backdrop, the Trump administration announced that Israel and Lebanon had agreed to implement a ceasefire and establish security zones in southern Lebanon, contingent on a complete cessation of Hezbollah fire and the full evacuation of its operatives from the South Litani sector. It was the second such ceasefire agreement in as many months; the first did not hold. Tehran has repeatedly linked any deal with Washington to an end of fighting on the Lebanese front, making the announcement diplomatically significant even if its durability remains an open question. Trump added to the noise in the US afternoon, suggesting a deal with Iran could materialise over the weekend. He was also reported to have told aides privately that he would not resume all-out war unless American troops were killed, a threshold that markets interpreted as confirmation the administration is tolerating ongoing skirmishing rather than seeking renewed escalation. The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution to block further Iran strikes, the first such vote since the conflict began in late February. The White House dismissed it and analysts broadly characterised it as performative, of no effect.. Asian markets opened Thursday in a risk-off mood. Seoul shares fell sharply and the Korean won sold off, while the Nikkei also declined, following a weak lead from Wall Street. Major currency moves were limited elsewhere, with USD/JPY drifting back below 160 by a few ticks without conviction in either direction. In China, analysts sought to soothe domestic market concerns, stating that liquidity conditions would remain stable and ample through June despite the People's Bank of China skipping open market operations again today, a communication exercise aimed at preventing the absence of intervention from being read as tightening. Away from geopolitics, the US Department of Agriculture confirmed the discovery of the New World screwworm in a Texas calf. The parasitic fly, which consumes warm-blooded animals alive, had been eradicated from the United States decades ago. Its reappearance raises serious questions about biosecurity and potential exposure across the American cattle herd at a time when beef prices are already near record highs. Still to come, RBA's Michele Bullock, Governor, Sarah Hunter, Assistant Governor (Economic), and Christopher Kent, Assistant Governor (Financial Markets) will all speak in Australia's parliament today. None will be dovish. due at 0500 GMT / 0100 US Eastern time Bitcoin continued to drop, hard, only finding a bounce under $62K: This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 高盛称，AI基础设施支出有望与全球最大经济体比肩 错过了吗？——科威特计划在霍尔木兹海峡重开数周内将石油产量恢复至70% 澳大利亚储备银行行长面临参议院质询，澳大利亚联邦银行预计连续三次加息后利率将维持不变 澳大利亚4月出口环比大增7.2% （前值-2.7%）澳新银行大宗商品指数因供应紧张而上涨，但新西兰元升值削弱了出口收益 中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8203（市场预期为6.7770） 比特币暴跌仍在持续。重回6.2万美元关口附近 《华尔街日报》援引官员消息称，特朗普告诉助手，若德黑兰杀害美军，他将终止与伊朗的停火协议 特朗普政府官员称，美国食品供应和安全未受“已完全控制”的螺蛳蝇威胁 油市快讯，油价小幅走低。以色列与黎巴嫩停火重燃希望。历史重演。以色列、黎巴嫩达成停火框架，但真主党掌握关键。国际能源署库存警告及原油库存大幅下降助推周三油价上涨。ICYMI——美联储持异议的洛根在沃什首次会议前对通胀发出警告。美国众议院投票反对特朗普的战争。该投票毫无意义。 摩根大通预计马六甲海峡将于6月重新开放，但未解答这一棘手问题 investingLive 美洲市场新闻综述：随着战争担忧情绪蔓延，美元走强 特朗普收盘后提振市场——称伊朗已接近签署协议 博通股价在财报公布后下跌 摘要：特朗普政府宣布，以色列和黎巴嫩已同意实施停火并建立安全区，但前提是真主党必须完全停止射击，并将其人员从南利塔尼地区撤离； 上月达成的停火协议未能维持 特朗普暗示与伊朗的协议可能在“本周末”达成，但有报道称他私下告诉助手，除非美军人员遇害，否则不会重启全面战争 美国众议院通过了一项战争权力决议案，旨在阻止对伊朗的进一步打击，这是冲突爆发以来的首次此类投票；分析师称此举主要具有象征意义 周四上午亚洲股市下跌，受伊朗局势再度紧张推动市场避险情绪升温，首尔和东京股市均大幅下挫； 韩元大幅贬值，美元兑日元汇率回落至160下方；其他主要外汇市场波动平缓；中国分析师表示，6月中国人民银行的流动性将保持稳定且充裕 尽管央行连续第二天暂停公开市场操作，但一种对温血动物致命的寄生蝇——新世界螺蝇， 这种对温血动物致命的寄生蝇，在得克萨斯州的一头小牛身上被发现，引发了对美国牛群的担忧。周三油价小幅走低，因美伊冲突传出的一系列矛盾信号令市场难以确定方向，特朗普周末关于达成协议的暗示，恰好足以缓解此前市场坚定避险的态势。当日的主旋律是升级与外交的碰撞，这已成为这场冲突的标志性节奏。前一天，伊朗袭击了科威特国际机场，造成数十人受伤，并引发了美国在霍尔木兹海峡附近发动军事打击。在此背景下，特朗普政府宣布，以色列和黎巴嫩已同意实施停火并在黎巴嫩南部建立安全区，但前提是真主党必须完全停止射击，并将其人员从南利塔尼地区全面撤离。这是两个月内达成的第二份此类停火协议；第一份协议未能维持。 德黑兰方面一再将与华盛顿达成的任何协议与结束黎巴嫩战线的战斗挂钩，因此，尽管该协议能否持久仍是个未知数，但这一声明在外交上具有重要意义。特朗普在美东时间下午进一步加剧了舆论喧嚣，暗示本周末可能与伊朗达成协议。据报道，他还私下告诉助手，除非美军士兵阵亡，否则他不会重启全面战争。市场将这一门槛解读为，美国政府正在容忍当前的零星冲突，而非寻求新一轮升级。美国众议院通过了一项战争权力决议案，旨在阻止对伊朗的进一步打击，这是自2月下旬冲突爆发以来的首次此类投票。白宫对此不予理会，分析师普遍认为该决议仅具表演性质，并无实际效力。周四亚洲市场开盘时风险偏好低迷。受华尔街疲软走势影响，首尔股市大幅下挫，韩元遭抛售，日经指数亦出现下跌。其他主要货币波动有限，美元兑日元汇率小幅回落至160关口下方，市场对涨跌方向均缺乏明确信心。 在中国，分析师们试图安抚国内市场的担忧，表示尽管中国人民银行今天再次暂停公开市场操作，但流动性状况在6月前仍将保持稳定且充裕。此举旨在防止市场将未进行干预解读为货币政策收紧。抛开地缘政治因素，美国农业部证实，在得克萨斯州的一头小牛身上发现了新世界螺蛲蝇。这种寄生性蝇类会活生生吞食温血动物，几十年前已在美国被根除。在牛肉价格已接近历史高位之际，其卷土重来引发了人们对生物安全以及美国牛群可能面临感染风险的严重担忧。后续动态：澳大利亚储备银行（RBA）行长米歇尔·布洛克、经济助理行长莎拉·亨特以及金融市场助理行长克里斯托弗·肯特今日均将在澳大利亚议会发表讲话。他们均不会采取鸽派立场。预计格林尼治时间05:00 / 美国东部时间01:00发布。比特币持续大幅下跌，仅在6.2万美元下方出现反弹：本文由investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹撰写。
items.summaryEn: AI infrastructure spend on track to rival world's largest economies, Goldman says ICYMI - Kuwait targets 70% oil output recovery within weeks of Hormuz reopening RBA governor faces Senate grilling as CBA sees rates on hold after three straight hikes Australian April Exports leap higher at +7.2% m/m (vs. -2.7% prior) ANZ commodity index rises on tight supply, but NZD gains clip export returns PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8203 (vs. estimate at 6.7770) The Bitcoin puke continues. Back near $62K Trump told aides he would end Iran ceasefire if Tehran kills US troops, WSJ cite officials Trump official says US food supply, safety, not in danger from 'fully contained' screwworm Oil update, price a little lower. New Israel-Lebanon ceasefire hopes. Here we go again. Israel, Lebanon agree ceasefire framework, but Hezbollah holds the key. IEA stockpile warning and deeper crude draw add fuel to Wednesday's oil rally ICYMI - Fed dissenter Logan raises alarm on inflation ahead of Warsh's first meeting US House of Representatives votes against Trump's war. The vote means nothing. JPMorgan sees Strait reopening in June but leaves the hard question unanswered investingLive Americas market news wrap: Dollar firms as war angst creeps in Trump pumping the market after the close - says Iran pretty close to signing Broadcom shares slide after earnings Summary: The Trump administration announced Israel and Lebanon agreed to implement a ceasefire and establish security zones, contingent on a complete halt to Hezbollah fire and evacuation of its operatives from the South Litani sector; a previous ceasefire agreed last month had failed to hold Trump suggested a deal with Iran could come "over the weekend" while reports indicated he privately told aides he would not resume all-out war unless US troops are killed The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution to block further strikes on Iran, the first such vote of the conflict; analysts described it as largely symbolic Asian stocks fell Thursday morning, with Seoul and Tokyo both sharply lower as renewed Iran tensions drove a risk-off shift; the won fell sharply USD/JPY drifted back below 160; major FX moves were otherwise subdued Chinese analysts said PBoC liquidity would remain stable and ample in June despite the central bank skipping open market operations for a second day The New World screwworm, a parasitic fly lethal to warm-blooded animals, was found in a calf in Texas, raising alarm over the US cattle herd Oil prices edged lower on Wednesday as a flurry of contradictory signals on the US-Iran conflict left markets unable to commit to a direction, with Trump's weekend deal hint doing just enough to take the edge off what had been a firmly risk-off session. The day's dominant thread was a collision of escalation and diplomacy that has become the defining rhythm of this conflict. Iran struck Kuwait's international airport the previous day, injuring dozens and prompting US military strikes near the Strait of Hormuz. Against that backdrop, the Trump administration announced that Israel and Lebanon had agreed to implement a ceasefire and establish security zones in southern Lebanon, contingent on a complete cessation of Hezbollah fire and the full evacuation of its operatives from the South Litani sector. It was the second such ceasefire agreement in as many months; the first did not hold. Tehran has repeatedly linked any deal with Washington to an end of fighting on the Lebanese front, making the announcement diplomatically significant even if its durability remains an open question. Trump added to the noise in the US afternoon, suggesting a deal with Iran could materialise over the weekend. He was also reported to have told aides privately that he would not resume all-out war unless American troops were killed, a threshold that markets interpreted as confirmation the administration is tolerating ongoing skirmishing rather than seeking renewed escalation. The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution to block further Iran strikes, the first such vote since the conflict began in late February. The White House dismissed it and analysts broadly characterised it as performative, of no effect.. Asian markets opened Thursday in a risk-off mood. Seoul shares fell sharply and the Korean won sold off, while the Nikkei also declined, following a weak lead from Wall Street. Major currency moves were limited elsewhere, with USD/JPY drifting back below 160 by a few ticks without conviction in either direction. In China, analysts sought to soothe domestic market concerns, stating that liquidity conditions would remain stable and ample through June despite the People's Bank of China skipping open market operations again today, a communication exercise aimed at preventing the absence of intervention from being read as tightening. Away from geopolitics, the US Department of Agriculture confirmed the discovery of the New World screwworm in a Texas calf. The parasitic fly, which consumes warm-blooded animals alive, had been eradicated from the United States decades ago. Its reappearance raises serious questions about biosecurity and potential exposure across the American cattle herd at a time when beef prices are already near record highs. Still to come, RBA's Michele Bullock, Governor, Sarah Hunter, Assistant Governor (Economic), and Christopher Kent, Assistant Governor (Financial Markets) will all speak in Australia's parliament today. None will be dovish. due at 0500 GMT / 0100 US Eastern time Bitcoin continued to drop, hard, only finding a bounce under $62K: This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive European session wrap: Trump lifts the mood but tech shares under pressure
items.summary: Headlines: Trump: The US is in the middle of final negotiations to end the Iran war Israeli military warns that the fighting in southern Lebanon continues AI drives tech sector to cut jobs by the most since 2023 BOJ reportedly expected to raise interest rates later this month - report RBA governor Bullock: We expect inflation to increase further in the near-term Euro area retail sales fall in April, challenging outlook ahead German construction activity experiences another steep rate of decline in May UK construction output falls at fastest pace for six years in May Swiss inflation holds steadier in May, core estimate remains subdued Markets: WTI crude down nearly 4% to $96.50 US dollar lower across the board European indices mostly higher but US futures down as tech shares drop S&P 500 futures down 0.4%, Nasdaq futures down 1.2% US 10-year yields down 3 bps to 4.46% Gold up 1.6% to $4,501 Bitcoin down 3.5% to $62,642 Another day, another case of the boy who cried wolf. US president Trump once again reaffirmed that a "deal to end the war" is close and once again markets are falling for it hook, line, and sinker. Oil prices are under pressure with WTI crude down nearly 4% to $92.50 on the day. Meanwhile, the US dollar is dropping across the board on renewed optimism in broader markets. EUR/USD is up 0.4% to 1.1640 with USD/CHF down 0.5% to 0.7875 on the day. The movement in the equities space is arguably the most intriguing though. European indices are holding higher for the most part and so are Dow futures, with the latter up 0.8%. However, there's some ugly selling going on in tech shares and that's something to take notice ahead of the Wall Street open later. Nasdaq futures are down 1.2% and that is leading S&P 500 futures to be down 0.4% as well currently. The declines are led by Broadcomm, whose shares are down ~15% in pre-market. And we're also seeing AMD shares be down ~4% and Micron shares down ~7% in pre-market, with Nvidia also down ~1% for now. The AI trade is certainly taking a hit this week as investors start to feel the pinch of the transition in wanting to see the numbers back up the costs. It's a case of show me the money time. In the bond market, we're seeing yields drop back with 10-year Treasury yields down 3 bps to 4.46% and 30-year yields down 3 bps to 4.96% on the day. Meanwhile, precious metals are also moving up with gold sitting higher by 1.6% to $4,501 and silver up nearly 2% to $74.10 on the day. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 头条新闻：特朗普：美国正就结束伊朗战争进行最终谈判 以色列军方警告称黎巴嫩南部的战斗仍在继续 人工智能推动科技行业裁员规模创2023年以来之最 据报道，日本央行预计将于本月晚些时候加息——报道称 澳大利亚储备银行行长布洛克： 我们预计短期内通胀将进一步上升 欧元区4月零售额下滑，未来前景面临挑战 德国5月建筑业活动再次大幅下滑 英国5月建筑业产出降幅创六年新高 瑞士5月通胀趋稳，核心通胀率仍处于低位 市场： WTI原油下跌近4%至96.50美元 美元汇率全面走低 欧洲股指普遍上涨，但受科技股拖累美股期货下跌 标普500期货下跌0.4%， 纳斯达克期货下跌1.2% 美国10年期国债收益率下跌3个基点至4.46% 金价上涨1.6%至4,501美元 比特币下跌3.5%至62,642美元又是一天，又是一出“狼来了”的戏码。美国总统特朗普再次重申“结束战争的协议”近在咫尺，而市场却再次对此深信不疑。 油价承压，西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）当日下跌近4%，至92.50美元。与此同时，受大盘市场乐观情绪重燃影响，美元全面走低。欧元兑美元上涨0.4%至1.1640，美元兑瑞郎则下跌0.5%至0.7875。不过，股市的走势或许才是最引人注目的。欧洲主要股指大多维持涨势，道指期货亦然，涨幅达0.8%。然而，科技股正遭遇猛烈抛售，这值得在稍后华尔街开盘前予以关注。纳斯达克期货下跌1.2%，目前也拖累标普500期货下跌0.4%。领跌的是博通（Broadcom），其股价在盘前交易中下跌约15%。此外，AMD股价在盘前下跌约4%，美光（Micron）下跌约7%，英伟达（Nvidia）目前也下跌约1%。随着投资者开始感受到转型带来的压力，希望看到业绩数据能支撑高昂成本，本周人工智能板块无疑遭受重创。现在正是“用业绩说话”的时候。 在债券市场上，我们看到收益率出现回落，10年期国债收益率当日下跌3个基点至4.46%，30年期国债收益率下跌3个基点至4.96%。与此同时，贵金属价格也在上涨，黄金当日上涨1.6%至4,501美元，白银上涨近2%至74.10美元。本文由investinglive.com的Justin Low撰写。
